Our Mission
Chapter 00 / The Mission

In order to put pro-grade sports analytics in the hands of every coach, athlete, and analyst — not just the franchises who can afford a data-science department. We believe the edge that decides games should be available at every level of the game.

Chapter 01 / The Premise

Every front office on Earth is quietly racing to out-compute the next one.

A generation ago, pro scouts relied on a radar gun, a stopwatch, and a gut. Today, they're joined by neural networks that crunch thousands of variables per player per game.

Artificial intelligence and predictive analytics are rewriting how sports organizations evaluate athletes. Modern teams use machine learning models to identify patterns in performance and forecast future results — analyzing datasets that would take a traditional scouting department years to read, let alone interpret.

But this advantage has been locked behind enterprise contracts and seven-figure analytics budgets. Velocity AI unlocks it. One platform. Every level of the game. The same neural networks, biometric analysis, and video intelligence that power elite franchises — now accessible to any serious team or athlete.

of AI sports research uses neural networks — the core of Velocity AI's engine

of data feed modern models — all unified in one Velocity AI dashboard
4×
projected market growth in eight years — and we're building for it
Chapter 02 / The Market

A $23 billion industry. A wide-open door.

The global sports analytics market is valued at approximately $5.68 billion in 2025, and is projected to exceed $23 billion by 2033. Every league, every team, every major broadcaster is hiring data scientists.

Yet most of that capability never reaches the athletes and coaches who could benefit from it the most. Velocity AI was built to close that gap — turning enterprise-grade analytics into tools that anyone on a sideline can actually use.

4×
growth in eight years · Source: Grand View Research, 2025

Chapter 03 / The Platform

One platform. Five data streams. Infinite clarity.

01 · THE ENGINE

Neural networks lead the field — and power Velocity AI.

Across peer-reviewed sports analytics research, neural networks are the most commonly deployed AI model (32%), followed by decision trees and random forests (24%), support vector machines (18%), and deep learning models (15%). Velocity AI combines all of them — deployed through a single API, unified under one intuitive interface.

02 · CAREER MODELING

Forecasting careers, not just games.

By analyzing thousands of data points, Velocity AI identifies development trends and estimates potential career outcomes — the same frontier the pros are racing toward.

03 · FIVE INPUTS

Every signal, unified.

Performance stats, GPS tracking, biometrics, video analysis, and historical data — all flowing into a single dashboard built for real decisions.

04 · BUILT FOR HUMANS

We don't replace your coaches. We arm them.

Velocity AI was designed around a truth the best organizations already know: AI alone doesn't win games. Our tools surface the insights — leadership, team fit, and context stay where they belong, with the people who know the sport. The model talks. The coach decides.

Figure 03 · Types of data Velocity AI processes in real time

Data Type Example Purpose
Performance StatisticsGoals, assists, yardsEvaluate player productivity
Player TrackingGPS movementAnalyze positioning and speed
BiometricHeart rate, workloadMonitor athlete health
Video AnalysisComputer vision trackingAnalyze tactics and gameplay
Historical PerformancePrevious seasonsPredict future performance
Chapter 04 / The Honest Part

What AI can't do. We'll tell you anyway.

No sports analytics tool is magic. Velocity AI is built on honesty about its limits — because that's the only way to build trust with the people using it.

LIMITATION 01

Algorithmic bias

Models trained on incomplete or biased historical data can reinforce existing inequalities. Velocity AI audits its training sets continuously and publishes what it finds.

LIMITATION 02

Overfitting

Algorithms that fit past data too tightly miss future reality. Injuries, coaching changes, and team dynamics don't live in a spreadsheet — and we don't pretend they do.

LIMITATION 03

Intangibles remain intangible

Leadership. Character. Team fit. These are the qualities that win championships, and no model can measure them. Velocity AI supports human judgment — it does not replace it.

Chapter 08 / References

The research behind the pitch.

Every claim on this page is grounded in peer-reviewed research or recognized industry reporting. Sources are listed below in APA format — click any link to read the original.

  1. Grand View Research. (2025). Sports analytics market size & share industry report, 2033. Grand View Research. grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/sports-analytics-market
  2. Gent, E. (2018, June 14). How AI is helping sports teams scout star players. NBC News. nbcnews.com/…/ncna882516
  3. Leite, T. V. P. P. (2025). AI-based sports scouting project management (Order No. 32366884). ProQuest One Academic. (3270824007). proquest.com/…/3270824007
  4. Bartlett, R. (2006, December 15). Artificial intelligence in sports biomechanics: New dawn or false hope? Journal of Sports Science & Medicine. pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3861744
  5. Zhou, D. (n.d.). Artificial intelligence in sport: A narrative review of applications, challenges and future trends. Journal of Sports Sciences. Taylor & Francis. tandfonline.com/…/02640414.2025.2518694
  6. Hammes, F., Hagg, A., Asteroth, A., & Link, D. (2026, February 22). Artificial intelligence in elite sports — A narrative review of success stories and challenges. Frontiers. frontiersin.org/…/fspor.2022.861466/full
  7. Pietraszewski, P., Terbalyan, A., Roczniok, R., Maszczyk, A., Ornowski, K., Manilewska, D., Kuliś, S., Zając, A., & Golaś, A. (2025, June 27). The role of artificial intelligence in sports analytics: A systematic review and meta-analysis of performance trends. MDPI. mdpi.com/2076-3417/15/13/7254
  8. Pisaniello, A. (2024, November 6). The game changer: How artificial intelligence is transforming sports. Paradigm. reference-global.com/article/10.2478/gssfj-2024-0006
  9. Gao, Y. (2025, April 30). The role of artificial intelligence in enhancing sports education and public health in higher education. Frontiers in Public Health. pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12075118
  10. Xu, T. (2025, February 15). Reshaping the future of sports with artificial intelligence: Challenges and opportunities. ScienceDirect. sciencedirect.com/…/S0952197624020712
  11. Mateus, N., Abade, E., Coutinho, D., Gómez, M.-Á., Peñas, C. L., & Sampaio, J. (2024, December 29). Empowering the sports scientist with artificial intelligence in training, performance, and health management. Sensors (Basel). pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11723022
